data.title: Senate Committee Postpones Crypto Bill Markup Following Coinbase Pushback
data.description:
The Senate Banking Committee delayed its crypto market structure markup after Coinbase withdrew support, prolonging talks over how digital assets should be regulated.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/senate-banking-committee-delays-crypto-bill-markup-coinbase-pushback/

data.title: Analysts: The contract market has shown "deleveraging signals" over the past three months, and the reshaping of the market structure may indicate the formation of a bottom.
data.description: CryptoQuant, a cryptocurrency data platform, stated that open interest in Bitcoin derivatives has been declining over the past three months, indicating a gradual decrease in market leverage, which creates conditions for a bullish shift in the overall market structure. Since October of last year, Bitcoin derivatives open interest (OI) has fallen by 31%, a "deleveraging signal" that helps eliminate excessive leverage accumulated in the market. The platform cited crypto analyst "Darkfost" as saying, "Historical data shows that such deleveraging phases often mark the formation of important bottoms, effectively resetting the market structure and building a more solid foundation for a potential bull market recovery." The analyst believes this pattern may be repeating itself, but added that if Bitcoin continues to fall and fully enters a bear market, "open interest may shrink further, meaning a deeper deleveraging adjustment and continuation of the corrective trend."

data.title: Bitcoin open interest falls 30%, setting up bullish recovery: Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin derivatives open interest fell 30% from October highs as deleveraging purged excess leverage, historically signaling market bottoms and recovery.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-oi-falls-30-resetting-markets-bullish-recovery-cryptoquant?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: UK Central Bank Considers Deposit Protection for Stablecoins
data.description:
According to PANews, the Bank of England's Deputy Governor, Dave Ramsden, has indicated that the UK may need to provide a protection mechanism for stablecoin deposits similar to that of bank deposits. Ramsden highlighted the central bank's consideration of maintaining public trust in currency in the event of a collapse of a systemically important stablecoin. He suggested that long-term trust in stablecoins might require a plan akin to bank deposit insurance, ensuring stablecoin holders have priority creditor status in bankruptcy proceedings.

Ramsden's comments suggest that the Bank of England might extend current bank deposit protection measures to widely used stablecoins. The central bank has already increased the protection limit for regular cash deposits from £85,000 to £120,000 to safeguard against bank failures. The Bank of England plans to implement stablecoin regulatory rules by the end of the year.

data.title: He Yi responded to users' expectations for the CZ role: Diversity needs to be respected, and being down-to-earth does not prevent one from looking up at the stars.
data.description: In response to a user's opinion that "CZ should not only focus on creating memes in the cryptocurrency circle, but should devote himself to future technologies such as AI, human regeneration, and Mars exploration," He Yi responded on social media that diversity itself is an important value of the industry and should be fully respected.

data.title: FUN will soon be listed on Bitget PoolX, unlocking 8.33 million FUN tokens by locking up BTC and ETH.
data.description: Bitget PoolX will soon launch the FUN project, with a total prize pool of 8,331,000 FUN. The staking channel will be open from 22:00 on January 15th to 22:00 on January 21st (UTC+8). This round of PoolX will have two staking pools: a BTC staking pool with a total airdrop of 4,332,000 BTC and a personal staking limit of 50 BTC; and an ETH staking pool with a total airdrop of 3,999,000 ETH and a personal staking limit of 1,500 ETH. Users with a positive net deposit of BTC and ETH during the event will receive a 5% BTC and 8% ETH interest rate bonus coupon after PoolX ends. Furthermore, first-time PoolX participants who meet the net deposit requirements will receive an additional 10% BTC and 15% ETH interest rate bonus coupon. The deadline for net deposits is 12:00 on January 20th (UTC+8).

data.title: Bitcoin Sentiment Shifts Despite Price Rebound
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 15, market analysis firm Santiment shared on social media that despite a rebound in Bitcoin prices this week, social media sentiment has shown an intriguing shift. The volume of bearish sentiment has been increasing. As market trends often move contrary to retail sentiment, the intense fear observed over the past ten days could potentially drive Bitcoin back to the $100,000 mark.

data.title: A blockchain company plans to launch a $200 million tokenized water project in Asia, with the first stop in Jakarta.
data.description: Blockchain infrastructure company Global Settlement Network announced the launch of a water asset tokenization pilot program, planning to expand the project throughout Southeast Asia within the next 12 months, with a target size of $200 million. The pilot will initially tokenize eight government-contracted water treatment facilities in Jakarta, aiming to raise up to approximately $35 million for facility upgrades and expansion of the local water supply network. During the project's development, the parties involved will also test a stablecoin settlement channel in Indonesian Rupiah and gradually expand to more foreign exchange settlement scenarios under a controlled environment. Mas Witjaksono, Chairman of the Indonesian Globalasia Infrastructure Fund, stated that Indonesia possesses a large amount of tokenizable assets in the infrastructure and natural resources sectors, and the relevant model has growth potential. The report points out that the financing gap for water infrastructure in Southeast Asia continues to widen, with long-term investment required potentially exceeding $4 trillion by 2040. Some industry insiders predict that the real-world asset (RWA) tokenization market is expected to see significant growth in 2026 as adoption accelerates in emerging markets. Currently, the on-chain RWA market size exceeds $21 billion. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $175 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $175 million yesterday (January 14th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $81.6032 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $12.773 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net inflow of $43.4695 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.61 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $20.84 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.1%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.744 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $844 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $844 million yesterday (January 14th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest single-day net inflow with $648 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $63.11 billion. The Fidelity ETF FBTC followed with a net inflow of $125 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $12.31 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $128.044 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.56%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $58.117 billion.

data.title: Binance Alpha: Sport.Fun (FUN) airdrop will launch on January 15th. Eligible users can claim it using points.
data.description: Binance Alpha will list Sport.Fun (FUN) on January 15th. The official statement indicates that after trading begins, eligible users can visit the Alpha event page to claim an airdrop using Binance Alpha Points. Specific rules and application details will be announced later. Binance reminds users to follow official channels for the latest updates and announcements.
